pattern: Splitting the wildcard pattern into its three literal pieces reduces matching to finding, for every occurrence of the first piece, the earliest-following occurrence of the second piece and then of the third -- a two-pointer walk over sorted occurrence lists.
hints: Split the pattern at its two '*' characters into three literal pieces; a matching window must start with the first piece, end with the third piece, and contain the second piece somewhere strictly between them.
Find every starting index where each of the three pieces occurs in the text (an empty piece occurs at every index, including one past the end); sort each list.

For each occurrence of the first piece, binary-search for the earliest occurrence of the second piece that starts at or after the first piece ends, then the earliest occurrence of the third piece that starts at or after the second piece ends -- that greedy choice always minimizes the window for this starting point.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, shortest match found later in the string
statement example, wildcards at both ends
pattern is just the two stars, matches the empty substring anywhere
middle piece appears earlier than needed, greedy must still find a valid later one before failing
the only occurrence of the last piece overlaps the required order and must be rejected
first and last literal pieces are empty, only the middle piece matters

stress.description: random text with a pattern built from three random substrings of it

solutions.python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def shortestMatchingSubstring(self, s, p):
        star1 = p.index('*')
        star2 = p.index('*', star1 + 1)
        p1, p2, p3 = p[:star1], p[star1 + 1:star2], p[star2 + 1:]

        def find_all(text, pat):
            if pat == '':
                return list(range(len(text) + 1))
            res = []
            start = 0
            while True:
                idx = text.find(pat, start)
                if idx == -1:
                    break
                res.append(idx)
                start = idx + 1
            return res

        pos1 = find_all(s, p1)
        pos2 = find_all(s, p2)
        pos3 = find_all(s, p3)

        best = -1
        for i1 in pos1:
            j = i1 + len(p1)
            k = bisect.bisect_left(pos2, j)
            if k == len(pos2):
                continue
            i2 = pos2[k]
            j2 = i2 + len(p2)
            m = bisect.bisect_left(pos3, j2)
            if m == len(pos3):
                continue
            i3 = pos3[m]
            length = i3 + len(p3) - i1
            if best == -1 or length < best:
                best = length
        return best
